author: Augustine of Hippo

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter XV. 15, 16.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is a worthy subject of inquiry how these words of the Lord are to be understood, “But I have called you friends: for all things that I have heard of my Father I have made known unto you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who is there that dare affirm or believe that any man knoweth all things that the only-begotten Son hath heard of the Father; when there is no one that can comprehend even how He heareth any word of the Father, being as He is Himself the only Word of the Father?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nay more, is it not the case that a little afterwards, in this same discourse, which He delivered to the disciples between the Supper and His passion, He said, “I have yet many things to say unto you, but ye cannot bear them now”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How, then, are we to understand that He made known unto the disciples all that He had heard of the Father, when there are many things that He saith not, just because He knows that they cannot bear them now?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Doubtless what He is yet to do He says that He has done as the same Being who hath made those things which are yet to be.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For as He says by the prophet, “They pierced my hands and my feet,” and not, They will yet pierce; but speaking as it were of the past, and yet predicting what was still in the future: so also in the passage before us He declares that He has made known to the disciples all, that He knows He will yet make known in that fullness of knowledge, whereof the apostle says, “But when that which is perfect is come, then that which is in part shall be done away.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in the same place he adds: “Now I know in part, but then shall I know, even as also I am known; and now through a glass in a riddle, but then face to face.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the same apostle also says that we have been saved by the washing of regeneration, and yet declares in another place, “We are saved by hope: but hope that is seen is no hope; for what a man seeth, why doth he yet hope for?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we hope for that we see not, then do we with patience wait for it.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To a similar purpose it is also said by his fellow-apostle Peter, “In whom, though now seeing Him not, ye believe; and in whom, when ye see Him, ye shall rejoice with a joy unspeakable and glorious: receiving the reward of faith, even the salvation of your souls.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If, then, it is now the season of faith, and faith’s reward is the salvation of our souls; who, in that faith which worketh by love, can doubt that the day must come to an end, and at its close the reward be received; not only the redemption of our body, whereof the Apostle Paul speaketh, but also the salvation of our souls, as we are told by the Apostle Peter?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the felicity springing from both is at this present time, and in the existing state of mortality, a matter rather of hope than of actual possession.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But this it concerns us to remember, that our outward man, to wit the body, is still decaying; but the inward, that is, the soul, is being renewed day by day.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly, while we are waiting for the immortality of the flesh and salvation of our souls in the future, yet with the pledge we have received, it may be said that we are saved already; so that knowledge of all things which the Only-begotten hath heard of the Father we are to regard as a matter of hope still lying in the future, although declared by Christ as something He had already imparted.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Ye have not chosen me,” He says, “but I have chosen you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Grace such as that is ineffable.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what were we so long as Christ had not yet chosen us, and we were therefore still destitute of love?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For he who hath chosen Him, how can he love Him?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Were we, think you, in that condition which is sung of in the psalm: “I had rather be an abject in the house of the Lord, than dwell in the tents of wickedness”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Certainly not.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What were we then, but sinful and lost?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We had not yet come to believe on Him, in order to lead to His choosing us; for if it were those who already believed that He chose, then was He chosen Himself, prior to His choosing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But how could He say, “Ye have not chosen me,” save only because His mercy anticipated us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here surely is at fault the vain reasoning of those who defend the foreknowledge of God in opposition to His grace, and with this view declare that we were chosen before the foundation of the world, because God foreknew that we should be good, but not that He Himself would make us good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: So says not He, who declares, “Ye have not chosen me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For had He chosen us on the ground that He foreknew that we should be good, then would He also have foreknown that we would not be the first to make choice of Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in no other way could we possibly be good: unless, forsooth, one could be called good who has never made good his choice.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What was it then that He chose in those who were not good?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they were not chosen because of their goodness, inasmuch as they could not be good without being chosen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Otherwise grace is no more grace, if we maintain the priority of merit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Such, certainly, is the election of grace, whereof the apostle says: “Even so then at this present time also there is a remnant saved according to the election of grace.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To which he adds: “And if by grace, then is it no more of works; otherwise grace is no more grace.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Listen, thou ungrateful one, listen: “Ye have not chosen me, but I have chosen you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that thou mayest say, I am chosen because I already believed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if thou wert believing in Him, then hadst thou already chosen Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But listen: “Ye have not chosen me.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not that thou mayest say, Before I believed I was already doing good works, and therefore was I chosen.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For what good work can be prior to faith, when the apostle says, “Whatsoever is not of faith is sin”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, are we to say on hearing such words, “Ye have not chosen me,” but that we were evil, and were chosen in order that we might be good through the grace of Him who chose us?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is not by grace, if merit preceded: but it is of grace: and therefore that grace did not find, but effected the merit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: See then, beloved, how it is that He chooseth not the good, but maketh those whom He has chosen good.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have chosen you,” He saith, “and appointed you that ye should go and bring forth fruit, and [that] your fruit should remain.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And is not that the fruit, whereof He had already said, “Without me ye can do nothing”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He hath chosen therefore, and appointed that we should go and bring forth fruit; and no fruit, accordingly, had we to induce His choice of us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “That ye should go,” He said, “and bring forth fruit.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We go to bring forth, and He Himself is the way wherein we go, and wherein He hath appointed us to go.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And so His mercy hath anticipated us in all.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “And that your fruit,” He saith, “should remain; that whatsoever ye shall ask of the Father in my name, He may give it you.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Accordingly let love remain; for He Himself is our fruit.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this love lies at present in longing desire, not yet in fullness of enjoyment; and whatsoever with that longing desire we shall ask in the name of the only-begotten Son, the Father giveth us.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what is not expedient for our salvation to receive, let us not imagine that we ask that in the Saviour’s name: but we ask in the name of the Saviour only that which really belongs to the way of salvation.
